replies.msg:
無意中睇到Quora有人問 : Why is there a big protest in Hong Kong?
有人答：
「香港人誤會左送中條例。」
This was why the extradition bill was proposed. It simply means that this murderer can be brought to justice by being extradited for his crimes. Seems fair enough.

The problem is that many people in HK seem to be misguided by what the bill actually is, thinking that promoting anti-Chinese propaganda would get them extradited to China. This is of course incorrect. Criminals residing in HK need to be assessed by HK law first before being extradited. This is the same law endorsed by the former British governing body, so everybody can agree that it meets international standards. If the severity of the crime exceeds HK terms, then those criminals must be extradited.

It seems that some people consider the bill to be a threat made by China, where in fact it was proposed by the HK government and its legal system. It really has nothing to do with China at all.



唔止，佢仲講話警察最初冇預到有咁多暴力情況，所以開始要用暴力：

What started out peacefully, became violent, and protesters have become rioters. The police were initially not armed as they did not anticipate level of violence that they would receive including bricks and dangerous objects hurled at them. They had no choice but to escalate the situation to putting up warnings and arming themselves:

仲話反送中係保護左殺人兇手：

Oh yeah, they’re fighting for the freedom of a murderer who sliced up his girlfriend, packed her into a suitcase and dumped her onto a river bank. Who is only tried for petty crimes in HK and is unlikely to be convicted for murder unless the extradition bill is passed.


已經有手足嘗試clarify，但唔夠人。
Quora好多外國人睇，要解釋返點解香港人開始以武制暴，送中條例可以幾醜惡。 7 月開 post到而家已經有2.1k個upvote. 唔好比佢誤導到外國人睇法，幫手答呢個問題。

推

香港自由之書——報應來了，街站收集投訴信
https://lihkg.com/thread/1510955/page/1

我由2012年開始用，一講任何地方/國家嘅政治就有班話歐美點衰，懷疑係因為反主流容易攞分。
轉戰其他討論區好啲。
我見到唔少師兄係攝影 Facebook Group post相都幾好。（我都有）
